### x-data Defines a chunk of HTML as an Alpine component and provides the reactive data for that component to reference. ```html
Content...
```

### x-init Allows you to hook into the initialisation phase of any element in Alpine. ```html
```

### x-show Provides an expressive way to show and hide DOM elements by toggling their CSS `display` property. ```html
Dropdown Contents...
```

### x-bind Shorthand: `:` Allows you to set HTML attributes on elements based on the result of JavaScript expressions. ```html
```

### x-bind:class

Shorthand: `:class`

Does not overwrite existing class attributes, and supports a special class object syntax.

```html
<div :class="{ 'hidden': ! show }">
```

### x-bind:style

Shorthand: `:style`

Does not overwrite existing style attributes, and supports a special style object syntax.

```html
<div :style="{ color: 'red', display: 'flex' }">
```

### x-on

Shorthand: `@`

Allows you to easily run code on dispatched DOM events.

```html
<button x-on:click="alert('Hello World!')">Say Hi</button>
```

Supports key and behaviour modifiers like `.enter`, `.prevent`, `.stop`, `.outside`, `.window`, `.debounce`, `.throttle`.

### x-text Sets the text content of an element to the result of a given expression. ```html
Username:
```

### x-html Sets the `innerHTML` property of an element to the result of a given expression. âš ď¸Ź Only use on trusted content and never on user-provided content â€” using it with user input opens you up to XSS vulnerabilities. ```html
```

### x-model Allows you to bind the value of an input element to Alpine data, keeping them in sync. ```html
``` Supports modifiers like `.lazy`, `.number`, `.boolean`, `.debounce`, `.throttle`, `.fill`.

### x-modelable Allows you to expose any Alpine property as the target of the `x-model` directive. ```html
```
### x-for Allows you to create DOM elements by iterating through a list. Must be declared on a `
### x-if Used for toggling elements on the page. Unlike `x-show`, it completely adds and removes the element from the DOM. Must be declared on a `

### x-transition Creates smooth transitions when an element is shown or hidden with `x-show`. ```html
``` Use directive stages (`x-transition:enter`, `x-transition:enter-start`, `x-transition:enter-end`, `x-transition:leave`, `x-transition:leave-start`, `x-transition:leave-end`) to apply your own CSS classes, or modifiers like `.duration.500ms`, `.delay.50ms`, `.opacity`, `.scale.80`.

### x-effect Re-evaluates its expression whenever one of its dependencies changes. Think of it as a watcher where you don't have to specify what property to watch â€” any property used inside it is watched automatically. ```html
```

### x-ignore Prevents Alpine from initialising a specific section of your HTML. ```html
```
### x-ref In combination with `$refs`, a useful utility for easily accessing DOM elements directly. A replacement for APIs like `getElementById` and `querySelector`. ```html Hello đź‘‹ ```

### x-cloak

Hides the element it's attached to until Alpine is fully loaded on the page, preventing a flash of un-initialised content. Requires this CSS:

```css
[x-cloak] { display: none !important; }
```
### x-teleport Allows you to transport part of your Alpine template to another part of the DOM on the page entirely â€” useful for modals and overlays. Must be declared on a `

### x-id Declares a new "scope" for any IDs generated with `$id()`. Accepts an array of ID names and adds a suffix to each `$id('...')` generated within it that is unique on the page. ```html
```

### x-mask

*Requires the `@alpinejs/mask` plugin.*

Automatically formats a text input field as the user types. Wildcards: `9` (digit), `a` (letter), `*` (any character); other characters are inserted literally.

```html
<input x-mask="99/99/9999" placeholder="MM/DD/YYYY">
```

Use `x-mask:dynamic` for masks derived from the input (`$input`) or the `$money()` helper.

### x-mask:dynamic

*Requires the `@alpinejs/mask` plugin.*

Generates the mask from a JavaScript expression evaluated as the user types. The current value is available as `$input`, and the `$money($input, decimal?, thousands?, precision?)` helper formats currency.

```html
<input x-mask:dynamic="$input.startsWith('34') ? '9999 999999 99999' : '9999 9999 9999 9999'">
<input x-mask:dynamic="$money($input)">
```

### x-intersect

*Requires the `@alpinejs/intersect` plugin.*

Runs an expression when the element enters the viewport. Use `x-intersect:leave` for exit. Modifiers: `.once`, `.half`, `.full`, `.threshold.50`, `.margin.200px`.

```html
<div x-intersect="shown = true">
```

### x-intersect:enter

*Requires the `@alpinejs/intersect` plugin.*

Alias of `x-intersect` â€” runs the expression when the element enters the viewport.

### x-intersect:leave

*Requires the `@alpinejs/intersect` plugin.*

Runs the expression when the element leaves the viewport.

### x-resize

*Requires the `@alpinejs/resize` plugin.*

Runs an expression whenever the element changes size. Inside the expression, `$width` and `$height` hold the current dimensions in pixels. Use the `.document` modifier to observe the whole document instead.

```html
<div x-resize="width = $width; height = $height">
```

### x-trap

*Requires the `@alpinejs/focus` plugin.*

Traps keyboard focus inside the element while its expression is truthy â€” essential for accessible modals and dialogs. Modifiers: `.inert` (aria-hide the rest of the page), `.noscroll`, `.noreturn`, `.noautofocus`.

```html
<div x-show="open" x-trap.inert.noscroll="open">
```

### x-collapse *Requires the `@alpinejs/collapse` plugin.* Smoothly animates the element's height when toggled with `x-show`. Takes no value. Modifiers: `.duration.1000ms`, `.min.50px` (collapse to a minimum height instead of zero). ```html

...

```

### x-anchor

*Requires the `@alpinejs/anchor` plugin.*

Positions the element relative to a referenced element (e.g. `$refs.button`) â€” useful for dropdowns and popovers. Position modifiers: `.top`, `.bottom`, `.left`, `.right` with `-start`/`-end` variants; plus `.offset.10`, `.noflip`, `.fixed`, `.no-style` (use `$anchor.x`/`$anchor.y` manually).

```html
<div x-anchor.bottom-start.offset.10="$refs.button">
```

### x-sort *Requires the `@alpinejs/sort` plugin.* Makes the element's children drag-and-drop sortable. The optional handler receives `$item` (the dragged item's key) and `$position` (its new index). Modifier: `.ghost`. Companions: `x-sort:item`, `x-sort:group`, `x-sort:handle`, `x-sort:ignore`, `x-sort:config`. ```html
  • foo
```

### x-sort:item

*Requires the `@alpinejs/sort` plugin.*

Marks a child of an `x-sort` container as a draggable item. The optional value is a key passed to the sort handler as `$item`.

### x-sort:group

*Requires the `@alpinejs/sort` plugin.*

Names a sort group so items can be dragged between multiple `x-sort` lists sharing the same group name.

### x-sort:handle

*Requires the `@alpinejs/sort` plugin.*

Marks a child element as the drag handle for its sortable item.

### x-sort:config

*Requires the `@alpinejs/sort` plugin.*

Passes a SortableJS configuration object to customise drag behaviour.

```html
<ul x-sort x-sort:config="{ animation: 0 }">
```
